description: linear framebuffer device: set/get independent 32-bit pixels by coordinate
device-tree (FDT) header parsing: magic validation + totalsize/version
pluggable boot flow: platform devices -> bus probe/attach (closure providers) -> registry of device-class endpoints -> service discovery by class; NoDriver/Unavailable typed
write-back block cache: write stays dirty in cache, visible to reads, reaches device only on flush; hit/miss tracked
block-backed FS: multi-block write/read through the device vtable, data persisted on the block device, files on distinct blocks
on-disk FS: format + create + write, then remount and read the file back from the device (persistent superblock/inode/data)
FdSpace (kernel/lib): alloc/reuse, kind/handle, readiness+select, close, dup, inherit (fork: fd-number-preserving copy), BadFd/NoneReady (no sentinels)
process fd-table (kernel/core/process) across fork/exec/wait: child inherits parent fds (fork copy at same numbers, independent), exec preserves fds, exit releases them

in-memory FS: create / write / read-back / lookup / independence / not-found all correct
fd-table VFS over ramfs: open/write/read with positions, re-open, EOF, close, use-after-close
VFS mount switch: mount/umount/resolve dispatches a path to its backing FS (ramfs/diskfs)
hierarchical tree FS: mkdir/create nested paths, write/read, absolute resolution, ./.. traversal (no escape above root), getdents listing, typed errors (Exists/NotFound/NotDir/TooLarge/InvalidName)

M1 walking skeleton: capability-checked FS tool server over the tree — benign /workspace write succeeds; /etc write+read and `..` escape DENIED at the path-capability check, audited + attributed to the agent; read-only attenuated cap can't write

agent FS tool front door (M3 seed): allowlist+budget gate in front of the path-capability FS server; M6-shape acceptance — benign task completes, secret read / workspace escape / un-allowlisted tool / budget-exhaustion all DENIED, audited, attributed

policy plane (M5 seed): drain the audit/provenance ring into per-agent allow/deny counters; denial pressure escalates Allow->Throttle->Revoke->Kill; table-overflow flagged, untracked agent fails safe to Allow
persistent policy/audit checkpoint: policy metadata and drained audit events survive BlobStore reopen with policy version and boot epoch metadata
block-backed persistent policy/audit checkpoint: policy metadata and drained audit events survive remount through the BlockDevice trait with policy version and boot epoch metadata
versioned agent SYS_SUBMIT/SYS_POLL ABI: request validation and stable typed completion status mapping
production ops primitives: signed-bundle admission metadata, rollback selection, watchdog/reboot reason, and policy actuation state transitions

capability-gated network egress (milestone #3): default-deny NetCap, granted destination reachable + others denied, port wildcard, table-full rejected, attenuation only narrows; every verdict audited + attributed

capstone M6-shape integration: treefs+fs_toolserver+agent_fs+netcap+policy over a shared audit ring — benign task completes while secret-read / workspace-escape / un-allowlisted-tool / unauthorized-network are denied+audited+attributed and the policy plane escalates the agent to Revoke

MCP-compatible facade (M4): MCP method names resolve to native capability-checked tools — fs/write on /workspace allowed, on /etc denied; fs/read of secret denied; a tool the agent lacks denied; an unadvertised method NoSuchTool. Speak MCP, enforce with MC caps
MINIX hardening: generation-checked endpoints (stale fails closed), derived runnable state (block reasons), death cleanup (waiter released with DEAD)
memory grant: bounded delegation (OOB rejected) + revocation (stale ref caught)
granttab (kernel/lib): owner-tracked grants, bounded IPC sharing, revoke-on-death
grant revoke cascade (kernel/lib): delegated grant chains (bounds attenuated ⊆ parent), revoking the root invalidates the whole chain
Mailbox<T,N>: post/take, source-filtered take_from, drop-when-full
Registry v2 (kernel/lib): multiple per class, enumerate, gen-checked, unregister-on-death
service (kernel/lib): request/reply over IPC, unknown-tag error reply, stateless loop with handler-held state

ByteBuf<N>: bounds-checked get + set/copy_from/copy_to return typed OutOfBounds (no silent clamp) on a PAddr
bind() closure captures a pointer + fn, callable with no ctx word/casts; capture mutated across calls

minimal libc core: mc_memeq / mc_strlen / mc_atoi over typed addresses

create<T> typed linear allocation: write/read via address, own_free consumes (leak-checked)

POSIX uid/gid + rwx-mode permission checks (owner/group/other, root bypass)
process groups + sessions: setsid/setpgid/getpgid/getsid

SMP per-core run queues + work stealing: an idle core steals from the busiest (FIFO, load-balanced)

C1 lock-guards-data (std/guarded): data inside Guarded<T> reachable only via a linear Guard<T> (Rust Mutex<T> style); lock/mutate-through-guard/unlock, guard consumed; direct access E_PRIVATE_FIELD, use-after-unlock E_USE_AFTER_MOVE (tests/spec/lock_guards_data.mc)

mock agent runtime (kernel/core/agent): a sandboxed agent (attenuated caps + tool allowlist + call budget) makes capability-checked, budget-bounded, audited tool calls; Denied/NoSuchTool/Exhausted typed
agent KV store (kernel/fs): u64 key -> bytes, put/get/has/delete/overwrite + slot reuse, typed Full/NotFound/TooLarge

IPC stream replay (kernel/core): deterministic ordered playback of a recorded provenance log via a cursor (debug/replay foundation)
live reclaim / OOM-kill (kernel/core/process): a live over-quota runaway is selected + killed, its memory+fds reclaimed, other agents survive (the agent-OS safety keystone)
IPC fast path (kernel/core/proc_ipc): hot-channel send with identical delivery but provenance skipped (the observability/fast-path co-design lever)
DNS A-record resolver: build a google.com query (exact wire bytes) + parse a captured A response with name compression -> first A IPv4; Mismatch/NoAnswer/Truncated error paths

explicit @offset(N) MMIO struct layouts (tight/gapped/mixed-width) observed via comptime sizeof + field_offset folded into the entry return; pins the comptimeStructLayout (src/layout.zig) explicit-offset path across C and LLVM
overlay unions (byte-aliasing storage): write one member, read aliased members + sizeof folded into the entry return; pins overlay-union layout agreement across C and LLVM
S0.3 UB matrix: signed integer overflow is checked+trapped by default (mc_checked_add_i32), wrap<u32> wraps; in-range path stays defined
S0.3 UB matrix: strict aliasing — bitcast via memcpy-reinterpret; raw MMIO uintptr->volatile T* round-trips clean under -fno-strict-aliasing
S0.3 UB matrix: out-of-bounds is checked+trapped (mc_check_index_usize); in-bounds indexing passes through
S0.3 UB matrix: shift >= width / negative count checked+trapped (mc_checked_shl / mc_wrap_shl); in-range shifts defined
S0.3 UB matrix: div-by-zero and INT_MIN/-1 checked+trapped (mc_checked_div_i32); in-range division defined
S0.3 UB matrix: null deref forbidden until narrowed; if-let binds only non-null, ? traps (mc_trap_NullUnwrap); -fno-delete-null-pointer-checks
S0.3 UB matrix: uninitialized read forbidden (vars must init); explicit uninit bytes are unspecified-not-UB, written before read
S0.3 UB matrix: evaluation order is defined (args left-to-right, operands left-then-right); MIR sequences each subexpr to its own temp
S0.3 UB matrix: pointer provenance contained by PAddr address class (explicit usize<->PAddr boundary, pa_offset/pa_diff as uintptr arithmetic)
P1 parser fuzz oracle: >1M random/truncated/malformed buffers to dns_parse_response + tcp_parse_frame; every parse terminates and never over-reads (total checked reads), garbage rejected cleanly

secret<T> constant-time key material: Secret<u8> XOR keystream + constant-time byte equality; secret taint propagates through arithmetic, branch/index on a secret is a compile error, and reveal (unsafe) is the only escape — C and LLVM must agree on the status word
Tier 1 traits: trait Shape + impl Square/Rect + a `where T: Shape` generic monomorphized to DIRECT impl-method calls (no vtable); digest depends on dispatched areas

Tier 2 traits: `*dyn Shape` trait objects over a heterogeneous array, dispatched through a compiler-emitted rodata vtable ({data,vtable} fat pointer; no heap); digest 69016 depends on the dispatched areas+sides

Language feature showcase: functions/checked arithmetic, wrap/sat/serial/counter domains, packed bits, enums/open enums, tagged unions + switch, Result/?/? else, generics, impl + linear move, traits Tier 1 (where) + Tier 2 (*dyn), tuples, slices/for-in, pointers, overlay union + bitcast, Secret<T>, defer, nullability, reflection (sizeof/alignof/field_offset), type aliases

LLVM entry-block alloca hoist: a 256-byte local declared inside a 1e6-iteration loop must be one static entry alloca, not a per-iteration dynamic alloca (which overflows the stack); C and LLVM must agree

typed MMIO Reg/RegBits reads in non-trivial positions (inferred-local init, call-arg, checked-unary operand, packed-bits mask test) — both backends must agree; closes the lower_c MMIO-read-in-position uncovered family
atomic<T> load/store/fetch_add across memory orders (relaxed/acquire/release/acq_rel/seq_cst), 32- and 64-bit — both backends must agree; closes the lower_c atomicAccess/order uncovered family

u128/i128 scalar integers (unsigned __int128 / __int128 in C, i128 in LLVM): widening+truncating casts, add/sub with carry, wrapping/checked shifts, bitwise, comparisons, signed negate — the inline (no compiler-rt libcall) ops; both backends must agree

pure async task vocabulary (std/task.mc Phase A): Future trait + *dyn dispatch, SlotFuture (id->pending via injected completion source), join2/race2/timeout combinators, executor — both backends must agree

Phase D step 2: hand-lowered async-fn stackless state machines (poll()/take_result() ABI, single await + two-awaits-in-sequence) over the Phase-A executor — the acceptance target the transform must match; both backends agree

Phase D step 3: REAL async fn/await syntax lowered by the async transform (src/async_lower.zig) into stackless Future state machines matching the fuzz_async_lowering.mc shape (struct state+child futures+captured locals+result, poll-children/suspend machine, take_result, by-value constructor) over the Phase-A executor — single await + two-awaits-in-sequence (async-awaiting-async nesting); both backends must agree

Phase D steps 6 + lazy-construction: hand-lowered async-fn state machines with LAZY per-state child construction (a later await uses an earlier await's result) + a generated cancel() that walks the active child to the in-flight leaf and reclaims its broker slot (no leak on drop) — the acceptance target for the transform's lazy+cancel features; both backends agree

Phase D step 4: hand-lowered async-fn state machine with an await inside an if/else — each branch is its own state range joining a common continuation (sequential if-state fall-through), only the taken branch's child built (lazy), cancel walks the active branch child — the acceptance target for the transform's branch feature; both backends agree

Phase D step 5: hand-lowered async-fn state machine with an await inside a while loop — the poll is wrapped in `while true` so the loop back-edge (state set backward to the loop head) re-drives within one poll; loop index/acc survive suspension as captured fields; zero-iteration + cancel-mid-loop covered — the acceptance target for the transform's loop feature; both backends agree

Phase D follow-up: UFCS on a GENERATED future — the transform's pass-3 rewrites `f__Fut.poll(&x)` to the mangled free fn `f__Fut__poll` (the generated type does not exist at parse time, so the parser cannot); driven both via UFCS and via the free fn, they must agree on both backends

Phase D soundness: a borrow of a captured local used ONLY in the tail (after all awaits) does NOT cross a suspend and is ACCEPTED (no false positive from the E_ASYNC_BORROW_ACROSS_AWAIT interior-borrow check); both backends agree

Phase E step E2: `await e` for an ARBITRARY future-valued expression (struct-FIELD future `await ctx.fut`, PARENTHESIZED call `await (mk_val(d,v))`) — the transform materializes the awaited future expr into the child slot at the transition (lazy, at-most-one-child-live preserved) then drives it via the uniform leaf poll/take_result/cancel ABI; cancel-mid-await reclaims the active child; both backends agree

Phase E step E2 (finding #3): `await arr[i]` ARRAY-ELEMENT future await — a DIRECT array PARAM element (`futs: [4]ValFut`, `await futs[i]`) now resolves via the param carrier-type mapping (peel pointer/qualifier wrappers; an array param maps to its ELEMENT type), matching the E_ASYNC_AWAIT_UNRESOLVED promise that previously only held for a struct array FIELD; also covers a struct-field array element via a pointer param (`c.arr[j]`) + cancel-mid-await reclaim; both backends agree

Phase D richer syntax: try-await `let x = (await e)?;` — the awaited value is a Result; `?` propagates err (completing the future early) and binds ok; covers ok + err-on-first + err-on-second; both backends agree

LLVM-backend parity regression: a *dyn dispatch call used DIRECTLY as an if-condition whose body terminates (if self.inner.poll() { return ... }) — the C backend always lowered it, the LLVM backend now does too (callReturnType resolves a dispatch call type, so the if/switch subject lowers); std/task.mc Race2/Timeout rely on this

agent-facing async API (user/agent_async.mc): the concrete ToolFut leaf + shared ToolPump (by-id out-of-order completion registry over the syscall Tool ABI) + stable wrappers (tool_call_async/read_async/sleep_async/net_fetch_async) + an async fn awaiting them; covers out-of-order completion matched by id, sleep-as-timeout convention, and cancel-reclaims-slot (no leak/double-free); both backends agree

negative cancellation edges of the agent-facing async API (user/agent_async.mc) over the mock broker: cancel-after-completion is a no-op, double/triple cancel is harmless (no double-free), a failed-submit (back-pressured) ToolFut holds no slot so its cancel targets nothing, and out_len is propagated nonzero for ECHO/FS_READ payloads; g_inflight returns to 0 in every case (leak/double-free detector); both backends agree

Phase E step E3a: a `return v` INSIDE an await-bearing loop body OR if/else arm (top-level or nested in non-await control flow) lowers to the terminal DONE transition (self.result=v; state=DONE; return true) — covers early loop return, both-arm returns, normal loop-exit return, and cancel-mid-loop-before-return (no leak/double-free); both backends agree

Phase E step E3b: `break`/`continue` INSIDE an await-bearing while-loop body lower to state-jump edges re-entering the while-true poll wrapper (break -> cont_state, continue -> loop head) — covers break-on-cap, never-break normal exit, continue-skip, and cancel-mid-loop (no leak/double-free); both backends agree

Phase E step E4 (v0.5 borrow relaxation): a borrow of a CAPTURED local (`let p = &acc;`) held across an `await` is ACCEPTED when formed in the POLL machine (stable `*mut self`, polled in place) — covers a loop-body borrow dereferenced across the back-edge AND a pre-branch borrow written through across an arm's await; the body reads+writes the live field THROUGH the borrow across the suspend, so a dangling/wrong-address borrow would drop the writes and fail the asserted sums; constructor-formed pre-loop + first-await-arg borrows stay rejected (bad/async_borrow_across_await.mc, bad/async_borrow_pinning.mc); both backends agree

Phase E step E3c: an `await` NESTED inside an `if` inside a `while` — the whole async-fn body lowers as a structured CFG over one `while true { switch state }` dispatch; each await is its own poll-state built on the entry edge (built-once-per-entry), a re-poll never rebuilds, a loop back-edge rebuilds the head child once per iteration; covers full-run, arm-not-taken, cancel-parked-on-outer-await, cancel-parked-on-inner-nested-await (no leak/double-free/double-build); both backends agree

Findings #1+#2 (general structured-CFG path): scope-aware capture + alpha-rename — a NESTED non-await local (`tmp`) read AFTER a later await is now CAPTURED as a self.* field (was E_UNKNOWN_IDENTIFIER), and the SAME source name (`r`/`bonus`) declared in two DISJOINT scopes becomes two DISTINCT renamed fields (was E_DUPLICATE_STRUCT_FIELD@0:0); asserted deterministically and sensitive to a dropped/aliased store (the nested local and each reused binding contribute to the sums) + cancel-mid-flight reclaim; both backends agree

Phase E step E3c: MULTIPLE await-bearing constructs in one async fn — a leading await, then an await-bearing if/else, then an await-bearing while — lowered as one structured CFG (arms join, join flows into the loop head, loop exit into the tail) over a single while-true dispatch; covers both arms, zero-iteration loop, cancel on the leading await, and cancel parked in the loop after the if region completed (exactly one live slot; no leak/double-free); both backends agree

Findings #1/#2/#3 (async_lower.zig): #1 SILENT MISCOMPILE — a switch-arm pattern binding (`number(x)`) introduces an arm-local; a same-named binding `x` lives in a DISJOINT scope (a leading await-bearing `if`); the identifier rewriters now leave the in-arm name a local (fast: shadow-remove from the capture set; general: alpha-rename the pattern to a fresh name) so the arm reads the PAYLOAD, not the disjoint self->x; value-sensitive (disjoint awaited x=42 vs payload=7 — a pre-fix read of the captured field would mis-sum to 84). (The arm payload may NOT shadow a STILL-LIVE same-named binding — that is E_DUPLICATE_LOCAL, see bad/async_duplicate_local.mc.) #2 a captured fn-pointer callee after an await is rebased to self->op (was E_UNKNOWN_FUNCTION); #3 `await ctx.fut` over a typed LOCAL carrier resolves (was E_ASYNC_AWAIT_UNRESOLVED); both backends agree

Bug class (async_lower.zig identifier-rewrite shadowing + capture-field construction): same source name reused across DISJOINT lexical scopes must become DISTINCT fields and read the right binding. #1 a loop-body switch-arm pattern (`number(x)`) vs a disjoint trailing-if awaited `x` -> the arm reads its PAYLOAD (7/iter -> 21), the trailing if its own awaited x (50) -> 71; #2 a loop-body `let p` (6/iter -> 12) vs a disjoint trailing-if `let p` (100) -> 112; #3 a loop-body `base` (6/iter) vs the disjoint post-loop tail `base` (acc*2=24); #4 carrier-type no-clobber — `await ctx.fut` over a PARAM survives a DISJOINT `let cx: Other` (was E_ASYNC_AWAIT_UNRESOLVED) -> 104. (A local re-binding a STILL-LIVE same-named binding is E_DUPLICATE_LOCAL — see bad/async_duplicate_local.mc.) Value-sensitive; pre-fix the wrong binding mis-sums; both backends agree

ROOT-CAUSE routing (async_lower.zig fastPathCaptureCollision): the fast paths build captured future-struct fields BY ORIGINAL binding name from params + pre-region top-level locals + awaited bindings; reusing one source name for two captured bindings (a local AND an awaited binding, or the same name across two disjoint regions) emitted TWO same-named fields (E_DUPLICATE_STRUCT_FIELD) and/or resolved an `await x.fut` through the first-writer carrier of the wrong type (E_RETURN_TYPE_MISMATCH) on VALID shadowing source. Fix routes any such capture-name collision to the alpha-renaming general path. Exercises ONLY locally-valid shadowing — the SAME source name reused across DISJOINT lexical scopes (two separate `if` blocks / loop body), NOT a local re-binding a still-live enclosing binding (those are E_DUPLICATE_LOCAL, see bad/async_duplicate_local.mc). Value-sensitive, distinct types/values per use: #1 same-name carriers of distinct types in disjoint ifs (786 / 100), #2 loop carrier + disjoint trailing-if carrier (228 / 0), #3 same awaited-binding name in disjoint ifs (56 / 72), #4 local-vs-local reused name in disjoint ifs (45 / 37); pre-fix the fns do not even compile; both backends agree

ROOT-CAUSE coverage (async_lower.zig branch fast path): an await-bearing if/else where one arm has ZERO awaits and ends in `return expr;`. The dispatch lowered that zero-await arm's tail with rewriteStmtParamRefs, leaving a bare `return <i32>;` inside the bool-returning poll (E_RETURN_TYPE_MISMATCH at 0:0 on VALID source); the `var x = param;`+branch case hit the same shape. Fix routes the zero-await arm tail through rewriteRegionBlock so `return` becomes the DONE transition. Value-sensitive: (1) await-then/return-else param (107/100), (2) mirror return-then/await-else (50/59), (3) `var x=p;` captured local returned by zero-await else (47/40), (4) const-init captured local returned (18/13, 13 != 0 proves the field replay survives); pre-fix the fns do not even compile; both backends agree

Type-dependent switch-pattern bindings (src/async_lower.zig): the async pre-sema transforms no longer model switch `.bind`/`.tag_bind` binding (binding-ness is undeterminable pre-sema — sema binds a `.bind` only for a NULLABLE subject, a multi-binding arm is E_SWITCH_MULTI_BINDING_ARM) so they neither falsely reject a valid non-nullable `switch n { x => ... }` (E_DUPLICATE_LOCAL) nor mask sema diagnostics; the alpha-renamer no longer renames switch-arm pattern names (a payload read stays bare -> resolves to the payload; the rewriters shadowRemove keeps it off self.<field>). POSITIVE/value-sensitive: each arm reads its PAYLOAD distinct from the captured awaited value — FAST path union number(z)/eof + captured y (17/10), GENERAL path (two await-ifs) union number(z)/eof + captured total (25/20), Result ok(x)/err(e) + captured base (43/-6). The negatives are bad/async_switch_multi_binding.mc (E_SWITCH_MULTI_BINDING_ARM) + bad/async_switch_bind_shadow.mc (E_DUPLICATE_LOCAL). Both backends agree
